description: Deterministic percentage-rollout bucketing: bucket = uint32_be(SHA-256(utf8(key) || 0x00 || utf8(subject_id))[0..4]) mod 10000. subject_id is the FULL wire-format id string (e.g. usr_<32hex>), UTF-8 — not bare hex, not the hyphenated UUID, not decoded bytes. An SDK feeding any other byte representation produces a different number and fails these vectors. Values independently cross-verified against the ratified ADR 0021 definition.

tests.description: Canonical vector: key 'new-checkout' + a usr wire-string subject lands in bucket 9557.
Same key, different subject lands in a different bucket (6848) — the bucket is a function of both key and subject.
Same subject, different key lands in a different bucket (2859) — the key domain-separates the hash.
An empty key is valid input and is hashed as the empty string, NUL-separated from the subject (bucket 1263). Guards the NUL-separator handling.
A non-usr subject (org wire string) buckets identically by the same rule (bucket 9985) — subject_id is treated as an opaque wire string regardless of its prefix.
Bucketing is deterministic and sticky: the same (key, subject_id) yields the same bucket as bucket.new-checkout.usr-0002 — no per-call variation.
Buckets are basis points in [0, 9999]; 9985 (beta-gate/org-0004) confirms values near the top of the range are produced as-is, never clamped or wrapped past 9999.
